title: Pic-Machina Image Sidecar
description: Per-image metadata sidecar file written alongside the source image (e.g. photo.jpg → photo.jpg.json). Combines workflow metadata written by Pic-Machina (EXIF mirror, user annotations, processing log) with rich AI-generated content analysis (scene, subjects, composition, lighting, colour palette, identified location, architecture, segmentation, generative prompts).

colorEntry.description: A single colour in a palette — human-readable label plus optional hex value.

hex.description: Hex colour value. Optional — AI may not always produce one.

boundingBox.description: Normalised bounding box with coords in the range 0.0–1.0 relative to image width/height. Optional confidence score and mask file reference.

x.description: Left edge as fraction of image width.

y.description: Top edge as fraction of image height.

w.description: Width as fraction of image width.

h.description: Height as fraction of image height.

maskRef.description: Optional relative path to a mask file (e.g. PNG alpha mask stored alongside the image).

$version.description: Schema version. 1 = original workflow-only format. 2 = superset including AI content analysis.

source.description: Immutable identity fields written on first save and never overwritten.

filename.description: Original filename of the image this sidecar belongs to.

sizeBytes.description: File size of the image in bytes at the time the sidecar was first created.

asset.description: High-level descriptive properties of the asset — what kind of image it is. Populated by AI analysis or manually. All fields optional.

title.description: AI-generated or manually authored descriptive title for the image.
examples: Skálholt Cathedral, Iceland — Exterior View from South-West

captureDate.description: Date the image was captured, ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). May be AI-inferred if EXIF is missing.

captureTimeApprox.description: Approximate time of capture in local time, as a human-readable string. May be AI-inferred.

analysisConfidence.description: Overall confidence level of any AI-generated analysis in this sidecar.

exif.description: Mirror of embedded EXIF fields extracted from the image. Read-only — populated automatically by Pic-Machina.

focalLength.description: Focal length as a human-readable string.

focalLengthEquiv.description: 35mm-equivalent focal length as a human-readable string.

shutterSpeed.description: Shutter speed as a human-readable string.

dateTaken.description: Date and time the photo was taken, ISO 8601.

gpsLat.description: GPS latitude in decimal degrees. Positive = North.

gpsLng.description: GPS longitude in decimal degrees. Positive = East.

gpsAltitude.description: GPS altitude in metres above sea level.

whiteBalance.description: White balance mode recorded in EXIF.

geo.description: Human-readable location derived from GPS or set manually. User-editable.

annotation.description: User-authored curation and editorial data. The primary fields a human edits in the Pic-Machina metadata panel.

tags.description: Free-form keyword tags for search and grouping. Human-authored.

usageScenarios.description: Intended use cases for the asset. May be human-authored or AI-suggested.

analysis.description: AI-generated content analysis of the image. All sub-sections are optional and populated progressively.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this analysis was last generated.

generatedBy.description: Model or system that produced this analysis.

computed.description: Scalar values written by Pic-Machina batch transforms. Backwards-compatible with the v1 'computed' section.

faceCount.description: Number of faces detected (string for template compatibility).

personCount.description: Number of people detected (string for template compatibility).

identifiedLocation.description: AI-identified specific place or landmark visible in the image — distinct from the GPS-derived 'geo' section. Useful for architecture, travel, and heritage photography.

name.description: Common English name of the identified location or landmark.

localName.description: Name in the local language, if different.

coordinatesApproximate.description: AI-estimated coordinates when EXIF GPS is absent. Less precise than EXIF GPS.

historicalSignificance.description: Brief description of historical or cultural importance.

heritageStatus.description: UNESCO, national, or local heritage listing status.

identificationConfidence.description: Confidence level of this location identification.

identificationBasis.description: How the location was identified — the visual evidence used.
examples: Tower form, rose window, staining pattern, hilltop setting, and Icelandic landscape characteristics

architecture.description: Architectural analysis of built structures visible in the image. Populated when the primary subject is a building or structure.

components.description: Named sub-elements of the building (e.g. tower, nave, entrance porch). Free-form object keyed by component name.

distinctiveFeatures.description: Notable or unique visual features of the structure.

scene.description: AI-analysed description of the scene and setting.

indoorOutdoor.description: Whether the scene is indoors, outdoors, or mixed.

weather.description: Weather conditions visible in the scene.
examples: partly cloudy with dramatic cloud formations

atmosphere.description: Mood or atmosphere of the scene.

style.description: Interior design or aesthetic style, if applicable.

settingDetails.description: Specific visual elements in the setting — interior furnishings, architectural details, landscape features, etc.

subjects.description: People or primary subjects visible in the image.

arrangement.description: How subjects are positioned relative to each other.

genderPresentation.description: Apparent gender presentation as observed in the image.

ethnicityAppearance.description: Apparent ethnic or regional appearance as observed. Provided for DAM and diversity-tracking purposes only.

visibility.description: How much of the face is visible.

framing.description: How subjects are framed within the image.
examples: building fills upper two-thirds of frame

viewpoint.description: Compass or positional description of the camera viewpoint relative to the subject.

foreground.description: What is visible in the foreground of the image.
examples: green grassy hillside slope and wooden fence

midground.description: What is visible in the midground.

background.description: What is visible in the background or behind the primary subject.
examples: dramatic sky with storm clouds left, blue sky right

horizonLine.description: Position of the horizon line in the frame.

skyCondition.description: Sky conditions observed in outdoor scenes.
examples: dramatic storm clouds left, blue sky right

highlights.description: Description of the brightest lit areas in the image.
examples: bright white building walls strongly lit on south face

colorTemperatureK.description: Approximate colour temperature. Integer for a precise value (e.g. 5600) or string for a range or description (e.g. '6000–6500' or 'cool-neutral ~6000K').

examples: hard directional shadows on left wall
slightly underexposed on subjects relative to bright window backlight

colorPalette.description: Colour palette observed in the image. Each entry is a label + optional hex. Three tiers: dominant (largest visual area), secondary (significant but lesser), accent (small pops of colour).

dominant.description: Primary colours occupying the most visual area.

secondary.description: Secondary colours — significant but covering less area than dominant.

objects.description: Notable objects identified in the scene.

tags.description: AI-generated keyword tags describing image content. Distinct from user annotation.tags — intended to be richer and more comprehensive.

generationPrompts.description: Prompts that could be used to recreate a similar image with a generative AI system.

detailed.description: Comprehensive multi-sentence prompt with full scene, subject, lighting, and style details.

negativePrompt.description: Negative prompt describing what to avoid (for diffusion model systems).
examples: text, watermark, blurry faces, distorted features, harsh flash

replicationSettings.description: Suggested camera/lighting settings to physically recreate the shot.

examples: low angle, looking up from base of hill

segmentation.description: Cached region and segmentation data derived from the image by AI or computer vision tools. Intended for AI-assisted editing (background swap, hair recolour, sky replacement) and advanced search. All coordinates are normalised 0.0–1.0 relative to image width/height.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when segmentation was last computed.

model.description: Model or tool used to generate these segmentation results.

subjects.description: Per-subject region data. Entries correspond by index to analysis.subjects.items[] where possible.

id.description: Subject identifier, matching analysis.subjects.items[].id if present.

scene.description: Scene-level region data for background and environmental elements.

ground.description: Ground, floor, or road surface region.

water.description: Water body region (sea, lake, river, pool).

dam.description: Digital Asset Management operational notes. Intended for DAM systems ingesting this sidecar.

identityNotes.description: Notes about identifiable people in the image and release status.

propertyReleaseNotes.description: Notes about property release requirements for identifiable buildings, artworks, or private locations.
examples: Publicly visible historic landmark; standard editorial use generally unrestricted. Verify for commercial use.
Private property — written permission required for commercial use.

brandingFlexibility.description: Assessment of how flexibly the image can be used for branding.

geoVerification.description: Notes on how location identification was verified, and confidence basis.
examples: Location identified with high confidence from architectural features: tower form, staining pattern, rose window, hilltop setting

limitations.description: Known limitations or uncertainties in this analysis.

processing.description: Append-only log of every Pic-Machina batch run that has processed this image.

at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the run completed.

asset.title: Skálholt Cathedral, Iceland — Exterior View from South-West

identifiedLocation.name: Skálholt Cathedral
identifiedLocation.historicalSignificance: One of Iceland's two medieval episcopal sees; ecclesiastical and cultural capital of Iceland for nearly 800 years from the 11th century. Current church consecrated 1963.
identifiedLocation.architecturalPeriod: Modern (1956–1963), replacing earlier historic structures on the same site
identifiedLocation.heritageStatus: Nationally significant historic site; contains medieval crypt and artifacts
identifiedLocation.identificationBasis: Tower form, rust staining pattern, rose window with cross motif, hilltop positioning, and characteristic Icelandic open landscape

architecture.style: Nordic Modernism with traditional ecclesiastical references
architecture.condition: good overall; visible mineral/rust staining streaks on tower face

features: Tall narrow vertical window slits (louvred bell openings)
Slate hipped roof with two dormer skylights
Rust/mineral staining streaks running vertically from window openings

nave.windows: multiple evenly-spaced rectangular windows in two rows
nave.additional: lower flat-roofed extension on front face

features: Gabled front facade with circular rose window containing a cross motif
Small brown wooden door at far right

distinctiveFeatures: Rose window with cross — sole decorative ecclesiastical element on exterior
Rust/mineral staining on tower from bell louvre runoff
Asymmetric massing — tall tower offset to centre-right
Completely white render against dark slate — high visual contrast
Hilltop positioning creating dramatic silhouette against sky

scene.weather: partly cloudy with dramatic cloud formations, patches of blue sky
settingDetails: Wooden post-and-rail fence at base of hill
Wild dandelions visible at fence line
No trees — characteristic Icelandic open landscape

composition.framing: low-angle, looking up from base of hill; building fills upper two-thirds of frame
composition.foreground: green grassy hillside slope and wooden post-and-rail fence
composition.background: dramatic sky — dark storm clouds left, bright blue sky with white cumulus right

lighting.quality: mixed — directional sunlight on south face, soft shadow on north face
lighting.skyCondition: dramatic mixed — dark storm clouds on left, bright blue with white cumulus on right
lighting.highlights: bright white building walls strongly lit on south face
lighting.shadows: soft shadow on north-facing (left) wall of nave

objects.name: dormer skylights
objects.note: small windows in hipped slate roof

generationPrompts.short: A large white modernist Lutheran cathedral on a green hilltop in Iceland, tall square white tower with narrow vertical window slits and dark slate roof, rust staining streaks on tower, dramatic partly cloudy sky, green grass foreground with wooden post-and-rail fence, summer daytime, wide architectural shot.
generationPrompts.detailed: Wide-angle architectural photograph of Skálholt Cathedral, Iceland. Stark white rendered modernist church on a gently sloping green hilltop against a dramatic mixed sky — dark storm clouds left, bright blue sky with white cumulus right. Tall square central tower with narrow louvred vertical slits, rust-brown mineral staining, charcoal slate hipped roof with two dormer skylights. Left wing: long nave with steeply pitched slate gable and two rows of rectangular windows. Right wing: gabled chancel facade with circular rose window containing white cross motif. Foreground: green grass slope, weathered wooden post-and-rail fence, dandelion flowers. No trees — open Icelandic landscape. Directional sunlight from right. Photorealistic travel and architectural photography.
generationPrompts.negativePrompt: people, cars, modern signage, power lines, distorted perspective, lens flare, overexposed sky, snow, winter, trees, urban surroundings, watermark, text overlay

replicationSettings.technique: low angle, looking up from base of hill

dam.identityNotes: No people present. No model release required.
dam.propertyReleaseNotes: Skálholt Cathedral is a publicly visible historic landmark; standard editorial use is generally unrestricted. Verify for commercial use.
dam.brandingFlexibility: high — clean architectural subject suitable for editorial, travel, and heritage contexts
dam.geoVerification: Location identified with high confidence from architectural features: tower form, staining pattern, rose window, hilltop setting, and Icelandic landscape characteristics
